evaluations.summary: The canonical contract intent is written as a bootstrap/run-specific statement instead of an enduring mission for the role package.
evaluations.evidence_quote: "Define the governed role for the agent-role-builder tool itself. This is the bootstrap act"
evaluations.why_it_matters: Canonical identity fields should remain valid across runs. Framing intent as a one-time bootstrap act makes the role package sound tied to this specific invocation rather than the standing purpose of the Agent Role Builder.

notes: The role markdown and role_summary describe a stable enduring purpose; the issue is isolated to the contract intent field.
